overall.description: A ship bridge simulation at night showing forward navigation through calm coastal waters. The vessel's radar and navigation displays are active as it cruises past a navigation marker, with the bow visible below and operational monitors overhead.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge with navigation consoles overlooking the bow

agents.ego_description: operating the ship's navigation systems while maintaining steady course through open coastal waters at night

entered.detail: dark metal framework of the ship's bridge with railings visible in foreground
left monitor showing coastal chart with yellow landmasses and ship position marker
center circular radar screen with rotating yellow sweep and range rings
right monitor showing blue depth readings and underwater topography
forward deck of the vessel with railings and red navigation lights visible below
dark calm sea surface extending to the horizon under night sky

segments.scene_description: The segment shows a stable view from a ship's bridge at night, with three operational navigation monitors displaying radar, charts, and sonar data. The vessel cruises steadily forward through calm waters, with the bow deck visible below and no immediate obstacles in the path.

events.description: white navigation marker buoy appears on the starboard side of the bow
navigation marker passes behind the ship's railing and exits view

agents.ego_description: maintaining course while passing a navigation marker on the right side

entered.detail: tall white navigation marker with reflective stripes visible on the starboard side

segments.scene_description: As the ship continues its forward progress, a white navigation marker buoy comes into view on the starboard side, passes alongside the bow, and disappears behind the railing. The radar continues its steady rotation throughout the brief encounter.

events.description: distant white vessel or landmass becomes visible on the horizon center-right

agents.ego_description: continuing steady navigation toward open water with radar monitoring for distant contacts

entered.detail: small white contact visible on the horizon, possibly a distant ship or shore structure

segments.scene_description: The final segment returns to open water cruising with the navigation marker now passed. A distant white contact appears on the horizon ahead, visible against the dark sea while the radar display continues tracking the surrounding area.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a steady forward progression from a ship's bridge at night, moving from a clear path through calm waters toward a distant contact on the horizon. It begins with a stable view of the bow deck below and three operational navigation monitors displaying active radar, charts, and sonar data. As the vessel cruises forward, a white navigation marker buoy emerges on the starboard side, gliding alongside the bow before disappearing behind the railing. The scene then opens back up to the dark, open sea while the radar continues its steady rotation. Finally, the camera tracks toward the horizon where a small white contact appears in the distance against the black water.
